title: umbilicus vulgaris subsp. gaditanus
intro: Umbilicus vulgaris subsp. gaditanus, also known as the 'Gaditan Orpine' or 'Gaditan Stonecrop', is a subspecies of the Umbilicus vulgaris plant, belonging to the Crassulaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Umbilicus vulgaris subsp. gaditanus include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and topical applications, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential benefits and interactions.
